statement.p: If both Pipe A and Pipe B are turned on, it would take 2 hours to fill a tank. If each pipe is turned on alone, it takes Pipe B 3 fewer hours than Pipe A to fill the tank. Answer the following questions:
If only Pipe A is turned on, it would take hours to fill the tank.
If only Pipe B is turned on, it would take hours to fill the tank.

solution.p: Lets understand how to use fractions to model work problems. Assume it takes a pipe 5 hours to fill a tank. If we treat the whole job as one whole, each hour, the pipe would fill \frac{1}{5} of the tank.
Similarly, if it takes a pipe x hours to fill a tank, each hour, the pipe would fill \frac{1}{x} of the tank.
Now we are ready to model this situation with math.
Assume that it would take Pipe A x hours to fill the tank. Each hour, Pipe A fills \frac{1}{x} of the tank.
Since it takes Pipe B 3 fewer hours than Pipe A to fill the tank, it would take Pipe B x-3 hours to fill the tank (Pipe B is faster!). Each hour, Pipe B fills \frac{1}{x-3} of the tank.
Its given that, if both pipes are turned on, it would take 2 hours to fill the tank. Each hour, they can fill \frac{1}{2} of the tank.
Now we can write an equation and solve it:

p.m: \displaystyle{\begin{aligned} \amp \frac{1}{x}+\frac{1}{x-3} = \frac{1}{2} \\ \amp \frac{1}{x} \cdot {2x\mathopen{}\left(x-3\right)} +\frac{1}{x-3} \cdot {2x\mathopen{}\left(x-3\right)} = \frac{1}{2} \cdot {2x\mathopen{}\left(x-3\right)} \\ \amp 2(x-3) + 2x = x(x-3) \\ \amp 2x - 6 +2x = x^{2}-3x \\ \amp 4x - 6 = x^{2}-3x \\ \amp 0= x^{2}-3x -4x + 6 \\ \amp 0= x^{2} - 7x + 6 \\ \amp 0= (x-6)(x-1) \\ \amp x-6=0 \:\: or\:\: x-1=0 \\ \amp x=6 \:\: or\:\: x=1 \end{aligned} }

solution.p: In the second step, to get rid of all fractions, we multiplied each term with the common denominator {2x\mathopen{}\left(x-3\right)}.
Since it takes Pipe B 3 fewer hours than Pipe A to fill the tank, it would take Pipe B {6}-3={3} hours or 1-3=-2 hours to fill the tank
In this situation, -2 is not a valid solution for Pipe B, since hours cannot be negative. x=6 is the only solution. Therefore, it takes Pipe A {6} hours and Pipe B {3} hours to fill the tank.
